identity.canonical_title: Single-Qubit Embedding from Sphere Address: ρ_i

theory.canonical_statement: on} F = |\langle \psi_{\text{sent}} | \psi_{\text{received}} \rangle|^2 \end{equation} \section{DAANSsphere-to-Bloch Mapping Integration} \subsection{Single-Qubit Embedding from Sphere Address} Let $\mathbf{p}_i = (x_i,y_i,z_i)$ be the unit DAANSsphere point selected by address $i$. Define \begin{equation} \theta_i = \arccos(z_i), \qquad \phi_i = \operatorname{atan2}(y_i,x_i). \end{equation} The associated qubit state is \begin{equation} \ket{\psi_i} = \cos\!\left(\frac{\theta_i}{2}\right)\ket{0} + e^{i\phi_i}\sin\!\left(\frac{\theta_i}{2}\right)\ket{1}. \end{equation} Its density matrix is $\rho_i = \ket{\psi_i}\!\bra{\psi_i}$.
theory.technical_interpretation: Single-Qubit Embedding from Sphere Address: ρ_i is an expectation / trace over state, written ρ_i = |ψ_i braψ_i. It is computed as an expectation value or trace over a quantum state, producing an observable. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
